message:
test(readme): AlignMode + DateOffset + date_range/bdate_range coverage

Self-review caught 3 uncovered surfaces:

- AlignMode: only Outer was tested; Inner/Left/Right untested
- DateOffset: only Day named in compile guard; no functional coverage
- date_range/bdate_range: in prelude but never invoked

Adds readme_align_and_date_offset_round_trip — reachable via prelude
alone — that drives:

AlignMode (4/4 variants on partially-overlapping indices df_a={0,1,2}
+ df_b={1,2,3}):
- Inner → 2 rows (intersection)
- Outer → 4 rows (union)
- Left  → 3 rows
- Right → 3 rows

DateOffset (3/3 variants applied to "2024-01-15"):
- Day(7), BusinessDay(5), MonthEnd(1) — all > base (forward direction)

date_range/bdate_range:
- date_range(start="2024-01-15", periods=5, freq=1day) → 5-element index
- bdate_range(start="2024-01-15", periods=5) → 5-element index

Real defect found: apply_date_offset (the primary use-site for
DateOffset, which IS in the prelude) was at crate root only — same
paired-surface pattern as fd90.10/11/12. Promoted apply_date_offset
to the prelude.

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.33.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.30","title":"test(readme): AsofDirection (Forward/Nearest) + DuplicateKeep (Last/None) + ConcatJoin::Outer coverage","description":"Self-review caught 5 enum variants across 3 enums with zero integration test coverage:

- AsofDirection::Forward (nearest succeeding match in merge_asof)
- AsofDirection::Nearest (closest match in either direction)
- DuplicateKeep::Last (keep the last occurrence)
- DuplicateKeep::None (drop ALL duplicate occurrences)
- ConcatJoin::Outer (concat with outer index alignment)

Plan: extend existing tests or add readme_minor_enum_variants_round_trip exercising each variant against frames/series with detectable behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:56:44.153158027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:58:51.859480436Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:58:51.857226186Z","close_reason":"completed: 5 minor enum variants covered","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.30","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:56:44.153158027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.31","title":"test(readme): CsvOnBadLines + ToTimedeltaErrors variant coverage","description":"Self-review: 6 enum variants across 2 enums uncovered by integration tests. Both enums have visible behavioral differences (parser mode for CSV bad lines; error policy for timedelta parsing), so untested variants are real coverage gaps.

CsvOnBadLines (3 variants):
- Error: raise on bad row
- Warn: emit warning, skip
- Skip: silently skip

ToTimedeltaErrors (3 variants):
- Raise: error on parse failure (default)
- Coerce: failed values become NaT
- Ignore: failed values pass through unchanged

Plan: add tests exercising each variant against inputs that exhibit the variant's behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:59:47.835035434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T13:02:37.547776661Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T13:02:37.545705087Z","close_reason":"completed: CsvOnBadLines + ToTimedeltaErrors variants covered","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.31","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:59:47.835035434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.32","title":"test(readme): RuntimeMode::Hardened + DecisionAction::Allow/Repair + IssueKind variant coverage","description":"readme_bayesian_runtime_policy currently only exercises:
- RuntimeMode::Strict (Hardened untested)
- DecisionAction::Reject (Allow + Repair untested)
- IssueKind::UnknownFeature (3 other variants untested)

The Hardened policy with decide_join_admission produces a decision but the test does not assert which action / mode appears in the resulting record.

Plan: extend the existing test to:
- Inspect the hardened ledger's record
- Assert mode == RuntimeMode::Hardened
- Drive decide_join_admission with a small row count (cheap join → likely Allow)
  vs a huge row count to exercise Allow vs Repair branching","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T13:03:44.031674681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T13:05:48.477576905Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T13:05:48.475452871Z","close_reason":"completed: Hardened mode + Repair + JoinCardinality covered","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.32","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T13:03:44.031674681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.33","title":"test(readme): AlignMode + DateOffset + date_range/bdate_range functional coverage","description":"Self-review caught these uncovered surfaces:

AlignMode: only Outer is tested via df.align_on_index. The 3 other variants (Inner, Left, Right) are pandas-parity surface for index alignment.

DateOffset: 3 variants (Day, BusinessDay, MonthEnd). Only DateOffset::Day(1) is named in the prelude completeness guard — none are functionally driven via apply_date_offset.

date_range / bdate_range: in the prelude since fd90.261 / fd90.269 but never called functionally.

Plan: add readme_align_and_date_offset_round_trip exercising each AlignMode variant on overlapping indexes (assert returned label sets), each DateOffset variant via apply_date_offset on a known timestamp, plus date_range and bdate_range calls with shape assertions.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T13:09:30.104178264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T13:10:07.615066064Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.33","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T13:09:30.104178264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.4","title":"fp-io SQL chunks index_col helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add chunked SQL read helpers that combine pandas-style chunksize with index_col promotion. Cover read_sql/read_sql_query/read_sql_table chunk paths by wrapping SqlChunkIterator, preserving per-chunk RangeIndex when index_col is None and promoting/removing the named column when set. Add umbrella re-exports and focused fp-io tests for query chunks, table chunks, None behavior, and missing index_col errors.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:11.325655162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:08:08.040272870Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T08:08:08.038384696Z","close_reason":"Completed in 90fd549","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","parity","sql"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.4","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:11.325655162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.5","title":"fp-io SQL options chunks index_col helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add chunked SQL read helpers that combine SqlReadOptions with index_col promotion for read_sql/read_sql_query paths.  {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.6","title":"fp-io SQL table columns index_col helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add read_sql_table_columns helpers that combine pandas-style columns projection with index_col promotion, including chunksize support. @@ -548,6 +548,11 @@ pub mod prelude {
         // match on Result<_, DateRangeError> from the prelude alone.
         DateRangeError,
         TimedeltaRangeError,
+        // fd90.33: apply_date_offset is the primary use-site for
+        // DateOffset (above). Without it in the prelude the user can
+        // name the offset variant but can't apply it from prelude
+        // alone — paired-surface defect.
+        apply_date_offset,
         infer_dtype,
         isna,
         isnull,
